description_long: This is a stylized biplane with a compact fuselage and two sets of blue wings, each adorned with white cross insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The fuselage is painted in a gradient of red, orange, and beige stripes, with a rounded blue nose cone and a wooden two-blade propeller at the front. The engine is exposed and mounted just behind the propeller, featuring visible cylinders and exhaust pipes. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in matching blue with white cross markings. The landing gear is minimal, with a small tail skid visible, suggesting a taildragger configuration. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, likely designed for a single pilot.
description: A colorful, stylized biplane with blue wings, red-striped fuselage, exposed front engine, and open cockpit. Features white cross insignias and a wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the blue upper wing with white cross insignias, striped fuselage, and tail section.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the blue nose cone, wooden propeller, exposed engine, and dual stacked wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the lower and upper wings, engine detail, and tail with cross insignia.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail structure, open cockpit, and the full span of both wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed cylinders.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit directly behind the engine, likely single-seat with no canopy.
